referential_task.1.player.chat_transcrip: [23:05:24] director: Basket 1 (top-left): a lidded wicker basket shaped like a curled-up animal (looks like a mouse/hamster). It’s oval and tan, with a small pointed “nose” on the right side and a little dark bead/eye; there’s a flat loop handle on top behind the head.  [23:05:37] matcher: ok next  [23:05:40] director: Basket 2: a dark brown, rounded bucket-style basket with a single tall arched handle. The rim/band at the top looks like a lighter wooden strip, and the body is tightly woven and bulges slightly toward the bottom.  [23:05:56] matcher: i have two that match that, can you say more  [23:06:00] director: Basket 2: the handle is very dark (almost black) and fairly thick, and it attaches at two points right at the rim. The basket body is a warm medium-brown with a slightly glossy look, and there’s a distinct horizontal wooden band just under the rim (lighter tan) before the darker weave continues below.  [23:06:17] matcher: how much into the basket can you see  [23:06:20] director: For Basket 2 you can barely see inside—only a thin shadowed opening at the top. It’s not an open display basket; the tall handle arches over and the rim is fairly high so the interior isn’t really visible.  [23:06:43] matcher: does it look like an acorn  [23:06:45] director: Yes—Basket 2 does kind of resemble an acorn: a rounded “nut” body with that lighter band near the top like an acorn cap, and the dark arched handle above.  [23:06:58] matcher: ok next  [23:07:01] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic basket in light tan wicker with a hinged lid that’s flipped open to the right. It has a central vertical handle rising up from the middle, and the open lid shows a darker interior.  [23:07:18] matcher: ok 4  [23:07:21] director: Basket 4: an oval, shallow basket with a single handle; the handle is wrapped/striped with light and darker bands. The basket weave is two-tone (light tan with some grayish/white strands), and the basket has a slightly pointed/teardrop shape on one side rather than perfectly round.  [23:07:29] matcher: sdhfs  [23:07:32] director: Basket 5: a small rectangular open basket with a high arched handle. The handle looks like twisted rope wicker, and the basket body has a chunky square weave pattern in warm honey-brown.  [23:07:32] matcher: sdjfhsjf  [23:07:34] director: Basket 6: a round lidded basket shaped like a cat’s face. Light tan wicker with two little ears on top, painted eyes and whiskers on the lid, and it sits on three small stubby feet.  [23:08:20] matcher: describe 4 in a different way  [23:08:24] director: Basket 4: a small “market” basket that’s asymmetrical—more like a droplet/teardrop profile, with the left side higher and the right side sloping down. It’s made of pale wicker mixed with gray/white strands, and the single handle arches over the center with noticeable light-and-dark wrap bands (almost like taped sections).  [23:09:43] matcher: are the handles made of like two smaller things that come together  [23:09:46] director: For Basket 4, no—it's one continuous single arched handle (not two separate side handles meeting). It rises from two attachment points on the rim and curves over in one piece.  [23:10:02] matcher: ok 5  [23:10:05] director: Basket 5 (again to confirm): a squat, rectangular open basket with a very tall handle made of twisted/wound wicker (looks like a rope). The sides have a pronounced checkerboard/blocky weave, all in medium golden-brown.  [23:10:27] matcher: does it llook like some rope is in the basket part  [23:10:29] director: No—on Basket 5 the “rope-like” look is mainly the handle. The basket body itself is a blocky, squared weave; it doesn’t look like loose rope coiled inside the basket.  [23:10:43] matcher: ok 6  [23:10:46] director: Basket 6: round lidded “cat” basket—face on top with painted eyes and whiskers, two small ears sticking up, and the whole thing is pale tan wicker sitting on three little feet.  [23:10:57] matcher: i dont think that is a cat  [23:11:01] director: Basket 6 is definitely an animal-face lid: it has a small rounded snout/nose in the center with a mouth line and whisker lines on both sides, plus two triangular ear shapes at the top edge. If it helps, it looks like a cat or maybe a bear-like critter, but the key is the **face + ears + little feet**.  [23:11:23] matcher: i only see two little feet, is that right  [23:11:26] director: On the target Basket 6 I can make out **three** little feet: one near the front-right, one near the front-left, and a third peeking at the back (partly hidden by the round body). If your candidate clearly has only two feet visible from all angles, it might be a distractor.  [23:11:45] matcher: ok the only one that seems to match only has 2 lil feet?  [23:11:48] director: It’s possible you only *see* two clearly in the thumbnail—one foot can be obscured. Prioritize these features for Basket 6: **round lidded animal-face**, **two ears on top**, **painted eyes**, and **whiskers** on the lid. If your “2-feet” one has those face details and ears, that’s the correct pick.  [23:12:08] matcher: painted eyes? this has like buttons or something  [23:12:11] director: Yes—on Basket 6 the eyes look like small dark bead/button-like pieces (not painted circles). The whiskers/mouth are drawn/stitched-looking lines, and the ears are woven triangles. If yours has bead/button eyes plus whisker lines, that’s the one.  [23:12:27] matcher: ok your instructions were kinda confusing then  [23:12:29] director: Got it—sorry about that. For Basket 6: **bead/button eyes (not painted)**, **woven ears**, and **a face with whisker lines** on a **round lidded** basket.  [23:12:40] matcher: k lets do 7  [23:12:43] director: Basket 7 (bottom-left): a rectangular basket with a single arched handle. The body has **greenish/teal vertical weaving** with **tan/orange wicker framing**; the top rim has a braided tan border.  [23:13:04] matcher: k  [23:13:07] director: Basket 8: a small squat oval basket with a single handle, in **gray/dark brown wicker** with **white/light tan horizontal accent bands** around the body. The rim is darker, and you can see the open interior.  [23:13:22] matcher: kinda like chocolate color  [23:13:24] director: Yes—Basket 8 is that chocolate/dark brown tone overall, with the lighter whitish bands striping through the weave.  [23:13:32] matcher: hm ok next  [23:13:34] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical basket (like a small hamper) in medium brown wicker, with **two small loop handles** on opposite sides near the top. The sides are vertical slats/weave and it’s open at the top.  [23:13:51] matcher: why are you using ** in your descriptions  [23:13:53] director: They’re just for emphasis (bold text) to highlight the key identifying features. I can stop using them if you prefer.  [23:14:10] matcher: its fine but its a little weird  [23:14:13] director: Basket 10: a shallow, round, tray-like basket with a single arched handle. It has a spiral-coiled pattern in the base, with two-tone light straw and slightly reddish-brown accents.  [23:14:22] matcher: ok i have two like that  [23:14:25] director: Basket 10: the handle is pale and attaches at two points on the rim; the basket is very flat and wide like a platter, and the center shows a clear tight spiral “snail shell” coil. There aren’t side walls much—just a low rim.  [23:14:25] matcher: is this one really wide  [23:14:27] director: Yes—Basket 10 is notably wide and shallow (more like a round serving tray with a handle) rather than a deep bowl.  [23:14:38] matcher: k next  [23:14:41] director: Basket 11: a deep, round tub-shaped basket in warm brown, with two thick side handles that stick up like small horns. The weave is diagonal/herringbone-like, and it’s fairly tall and open.  [23:14:56] matcher: is this basket like kind of dirty  [23:14:58] director: Basket 11 looks a bit rustic/rough—uneven color with some darker patches in the weave—so yes, it can read as slightly “dirty” or weathered compared to the cleaner lighter baskets.  [23:15:11] matcher: i actually see like 6 handles, are you sure its 2  [23:15:14] director: On Basket 11, it’s **2 main handles**—one on each side—each made of multiple woven strands, so it can look like several little loops/“handles” clustered together. But functionally it’s just the two side handles (left and right).  [23:15:28] matcher: ok next  [23:15:30] director: Basket 12: an oval, open wicker basket in light-medium tan with a single arched handle. The sides are fairly high and smooth, with a neat tight weave; overall it looks like a classic simple gathering basket (no lid, no color accents).  [23:16:03] matcher: give me more detail, i have a few like that  [23:16:06] director: Basket 12: the handle is thick and rounded and rises from the rim at two points; the basket has a slightly “boxy oval” footprint (not perfectly round), with the rim forming a smooth continuous oval. The interior looks uniform tan (no cloth liner), and the weave on the sides is very regular—almost like fine vertical ribs—with a slightly darker band right under the rim.

referential_task.2.player.chat_transcrip: [23:16:57] director: Basket 1 (top-left, Round 2): a rectangular wicker picnic basket with a hinged lid flipped open to the right. It has a single central vertical handle/strap rising up from the middle divider, and the weave is light golden-brown.  [23:17:27] matcher: k  [23:17:30] director: Basket 2 (Round 2): tall cylindrical hamper-style basket, medium brown, open top, with two small loop handles on opposite sides near the rim.  [23:17:40] matcher: k  [23:17:44] director: Basket 3 (Round 2): rounded “acorn-like” bucket basket with one thick dark (almost black) arched handle; there’s a lighter tan wooden band just under the rim, and the body is warm brown and bulging.  [23:18:04] matcher: lets call that one the acorn in the future  [23:18:07] director: Agreed — Basket 3 = “the acorn.”  [23:18:07] matcher: next  [23:18:10] director: Basket 4 (Round 2): small open oval basket with a tall handle made of twisted rope-like wicker; basket body has a chunky square/checker weave in honey-brown.  [23:18:25] matcher: ok next  [23:18:29] director: Basket 5 (Round 2): small rectangular open basket with dark gray/brown wicker and pale horizontal bands; single arched handle, and the rim/handle are darker than the lighter stripe accents.  [23:18:40] matcher: lets call 4 the twisted squareish handle  [23:18:43] director: Works for me — Basket 4 = “twisted squareish handle.”  [23:19:10] matcher: now 6  [23:19:14] director: Basket 6 (Round 2): lidded wicker animal shaped like a mouse/hamster—oval tan body with a pointed nose on the right, a small dark bead eye, and a flat loop handle on top behind the head.  [23:19:25] matcher: only one eye?  [23:19:28] director: Yes—only one bead eye is visible in the photo (the side profile), on the head near the nose.  [23:19:44] matcher: ok got it  [23:19:48] director: Basket 7 (Round 2): lidded round wicker basket with an animal face on top (cat-like). It’s pale straw color, has two woven ears, bead/button eyes, whisker lines on the lid, and sits on little feet.  [23:19:58] matcher: let's call this the cat  [23:20:00] director: Agreed — Basket 7 = “the cat.”  [23:20:04] matcher: next  [23:20:07] director: Basket 8 (Round 2): wide, very shallow round tray-basket with a single pale arched handle; the base has a tight spiral coil pattern (light straw with some reddish-brown accents).  [23:20:41] matcher: ok next  [23:20:44] director: Basket 9 (Round 2): asymmetrical teardrop-shaped open basket in pale wicker mixed with gray/white strands; single continuous arched handle with noticeable wrapped/striped sections.  [23:20:57] matcher: ok we got this one wrong before, can you give memore details  [23:21:03] director: For Basket 9 (teardrop gray-mix): the **rim is uneven**—one side is higher, and the opposite side slopes down, giving it that droplet profile. The handle is **not twisted**; it’s a smooth arc but has **banded wraps** (like alternating light/darker segments) along the handle. The basket body is mostly **pale straw** but with **distinct gray/white strands woven throughout**, making it look “washed out” compared to the warmer brown baskets.  [23:21:50] matcher: does it have like some red tape on the handle  [23:21:53] director: No red tape on Basket 9’s handle—just light/dark banding wraps (tan and slightly darker tan/brown), not a bright red accent. The one with red-ish accents is the shallow spiral tray (Basket 8).  [23:22:20] matcher: i dont think 8 is red at all  [23:22:23] director: Fair—Basket 8’s accents are subtle: more like slightly darker tan / faint rust-brown in the spiral coil, not bright red. The key for 8 is “very wide + very shallow + clear spiral coil base + single pale handle.”  [23:22:59] matcher: for num 9, when you say uneven, do you mean that its just pictured at an angle or is the basket rim actually squished or something  [23:23:02] director: The rim is actually shaped uneven/asymmetrical (not just camera angle): one end of the oval is higher and the opposite end is lower, so the opening looks slanted—like a teardrop/droplet form rather than a level oval rim.  [23:23:28] matcher: idk what you mean by teardrop, can you say something else  [23:23:32] director: Sure: Basket 9 looks like an oval basket where **one side is “pulled up” taller** and the opposite side is **pushed down lower**, so the opening is **slanted** (like a scoop shape). It’s also the one with **pale straw mixed with gray/white weaving** and a **banded/wrapped handle**.  [23:23:57] matcher: ok i am just guessing on that one, lets move on  [23:24:00] director: Basket 10 (Round 2): rectangular open basket with a single arched handle; the body has green/teal vertical weave panels framed by tan/orange wicker, with a braided tan rim.  [23:24:14] matcher: lets call this the green one  [23:24:16] director: Agreed — Basket 10 = “the green one.”  [23:24:22] matcher: ok moving on  [23:24:25] director: Basket 11 (Round 2): deep round tub basket in warm brown with diagonal/herringbone weave, and two chunky side handles (one left, one right) that stick up above the rim.  [23:24:48] matcher: is this the one we talked about before being dirty  [23:24:51] director: Yes—Basket 11 is the rustic/“dirty-looking” one (uneven darker patches, rougher-looking weave) with the two thick side handles.  [23:24:59] matcher: ok last one now  [23:25:03] director: Basket 12 (Round 2): open oval basket in uniform light-medium tan wicker with a single thick arched handle; neat tight regular weave, no color bands/liners/lid—just a plain classic gathering basket with fairly high sides.  [23:25:19] matcher: is this onekind of squished in the middle  [23:25:23] director: No—Basket 12 isn’t pinched/squished at the waist. Its sides are fairly straight and smooth up to an even oval rim. The “squished/uneven rim” shape is Basket 9, not 12.  [23:25:37] matcher: compare 9 and 12 please  [23:25:42] director: Basket 9 vs Basket 12:  - **Basket 9:** asymmetrical opening—one side of the rim is higher and the other side lower (slanted “scoop” shape). Color looks **pale straw mixed with gray/white**. Handle has **banded/wrapped sections**. - **Basket 12:** symmetrical classic oval—**even rim all the way around**, no pinched waist. Color is **uniform tan**. Handle is **plain thick wicker** with no banded wrap look.
